Assembly: On Aug 27, 2022 this sequence version replaced OX122894.1.; The assembly iyAndHatt1.2 is based on 49x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Scaffold order and orientation uncertain chromosome 1 61.37-77.87mb, 3 19.30-20.39mb and 29.90-33.14mb, 5 20.86-26.68mb.

Assembly: The assembly ihCypTris2.1 is based on 119x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. HiC and PacBio samples were from different individuals. Based on read coverage and assembly results the HIC sample appears to be a female and and the PacBio sample appears to be male. As such, the Y chromosome has not been been scaffolded as it is not present in the HIC sample.

Assembly: The assembly icDipFagi1.hap1.1 is based on 29x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Assembly: The assembly ilGraStol1.hap1.1 is based on 76x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).

Assembly: The assembly iyAndBarb2.1 is based on 53x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. A large number of centromeric repeats is in the unlocalised sequences. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 2 between ~54.25-55.12 Mbp is unsure. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 3 between ~47.57-49.54 Mbp is unsure. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 6 between ~17.81-19.02 Mbp is unsure.

Assembly: The assembly iyAndNiti1.1 is based on 71x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of scaffolds within the centromeric regions (chromosome 1 at 20.5-47.0 Mbp / chromosome 2 at 27.9-55 Mbp / chromosome 3 at 26-40.5 Mbp and chromosome 4 at 27.4-33.4 Mbp) is unknown. An additional 40 Mbp of centromeric sequence that couldn't be assigned to an unique chromosome is in the unlocalised regions.

Assembly: The assembly iyAndMinu1.1 is based on 36x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Centromeric regions have uncertain scaffold order and orientation; chromosome 1 19.91-47.34mb, chromosome 3 18.35-21.30mb, chromosome 4 14.98-22.41mb, chromosome 5 13.02-19.20mb, chromosome 6 16.99-19.07mb, chromosome 7 11.67-16.99mb.
